Monday: Margaret opened with "Jalapeño corn dog bites. We're doing it this week and I need to know right..." - questioning whether the dish had real merit. Marcus confirmed the cornmeal-jalapeño technique was legitimate. Stephanie proposed leading with the moisture-seal angle, which Margaret approved. Marcus will anchor copy around how the cornmeal crust concentrates heat on the bite.

Tuesday: Margaret confirmed a 60-40 cornmeal-to-flour minimum after testing - "Marcus gets it. Fine. Cornmeal-to-flour ratio is 60-40 minimum or the seal breaks down in the oil." Marcus framed this as a performance threshold, not a limitation. Stephanie agreed the copy should anchor on crust performance rather than ingredients. All three aligned on shipping.

Wednesday: Julian presented three bite-through shots. Stephanie pushed for shot two - the crisp exterior shattering - over Margaret's preferred jalapeño cross-section. Margaret agreed shot two works, saying "the crisp matters more than the cross-section." Decision locked: shot two leads.

Stephanie: "Shot two's the one - if people are stopping their scroll, it's because they see that crisp exterior breaking apart, right?"

Thursday: Marcus brought finalized copy focusing on cornmeal's moisture-seal function. Margaret pushed to lead with the steam-absorption mechanic - "stop explaining the why, just show what happens when you fry it." Stephanie flagged repetition: "Love that you went all in on the moisture-seal thing - let's see what you've got?" Marcus tightened the structure, leading with cornmeal arresting moisture, then pivoting to texture. Margaret approved.

Friday: Margaret opened: "Final review. Shot two locked, copy locked. Someone tell me what isn't right, because something..." - but the team had no notes. Marcus credited trusting the work over overthinking. Stephanie noted the team listened instead of defending positions. Marcus and Julian agreed restraint drove both the copy and shot two's success.

Saturday: This conversation contains no discussion of "Jalapeno Corn Dog Bites" - it is a technical deployment exchange. Devon confirmed verification passed and pushed the project live. Margaret advocated for restraint as a guiding principle, which Devon agreed with. No ingredients, techniques, or food ideas were discussed.
